provenance.note: verify r1 (level-gloss): reworded the definition off 'past' (which passed the gate only by colliding with the harvested topic-name 'Past') to taught-only 'Before now; not happening now.'

items.d: Before now; not happening now.
items.s: Long ___, there were many crocodiles in this river.
items.hintDe: Es zeigt, wie lange etwas her ist: 'Vor zwei Tagen …'

provenance.note: Carrier invented (v1 idea reused). SB9 fact file gives anaconda max length 6 m. Glosses 'snake' in def.

items.d: A very long heavy snake. It lives near rivers and can be as long as a car.
items.s: The ___ in the river was as long as a car.
items.hintDe: Eine sehr lange schwere Schlange, die nahe an Flüssen lebt.

provenance.note: Carrier invented (v1 idea reworked into in-level words; 'lions' and 'legs' are taught). SB13/15 list antelope as a fast animal. 'mammal' in-level.

items.d: A fast wild mammal with long legs. It lives in hot places and runs from lions.
items.s: The ___ has got long legs and runs very fast from lions.
items.hintDe: Ein schnelles wildes Säugetier mit langen Beinen, das in heißen Gegenden lebt.

provenance.note: Carrier invented (v1 'flew out of the barn' was above-level; rephrased to in-level). SB8 'BUMBLEBEE BAT ... smallest mammal' supports the mammal framing. Glosses 'fly' (s and d) and 'bird' (d).

items.d: A small dark mammal that can fly at night. It is not a bird.
items.s: A ___ can fly in the dark at night.
items.hintDe: Ein kleines dunkles Säugetier, das nachts fliegt — aber kein Vogel ist.

provenance.note: Carrier invented (v1 idea reused; 15 cm pencil). Masterlist exampleSb ('One centimetre is 10 millimetres.') uses above-level 'millimetres'. SB8 'It is 3 centimetres long' supports the unit.

items.d: A small unit for how long a thing is.
items.s: My pencil is about 15 ___ long.
items.hintDe: Eine kleine Maßeinheit für die Länge: 100 davon sind ein Meter.

provenance.note: Carrier from SB8 ('The fastest land animal in the world is the CHEETAH. It can run very fast'), rephrased to in-level words. Glosses 'cat' in def.

items.d: A wild cat with spots. It is the fastest mammal on land.
items.s: The ___ can run faster than any car on land.
items.hintDe: Eine wilde Katze mit Flecken — das schnellste Säugetier an Land.

provenance.note: Carrier invented, echoing SB10 'He's a chimpanzee, and he's as big as me' (as...as comparison). 'mammal' and 'monkey' in-level; no gloss needed.

items.d: A very clever mammal, like a big monkey.
items.s: The ___ at the zoo is as clever as a small child.
items.hintDe: Ein sehr kluges Säugetier, ähnlich wie ein großer Affe.

provenance.note: Carrier verbatim from masterlist exampleSb ('Many animals die because of climate change.'); also SB6 ('golden toads died out because of climate change'). Glosses 'animals' (s), 'world' and 'warmer' (d). MC foils are in-bank single nouns.

items.d: When the weather of the world becomes warmer.
items.s: Many animals die because of ___.
items.hintDe: Wenn das Wetter der Welt langsam wärmer wird.

provenance.note: Carrier adapted from SB reading ('the croc on the bank of the river ... it opened its big mouth') into in-level words. Glosses 'reptile' in def.

items.d: A big green reptile with long teeth. It lives near rivers in hot places.
items.s: The big green ___ was next to the river with its mouth open.
items.hintDe: Ein großes grünes Reptil mit langen Zähnen, das nahe an Flüssen lebt.

provenance.note: Carrier from v1 idea, fully in-level. Masterlist exampleSb ('The taipan snake can be very dangerous.') uses above-level 'taipan'/'snake', so road example used instead.

items.d: This can hurt you or make you ill.
items.s: Playing on the road is very ___.
items.hintDe: Etwas, das dir wehtun oder dich krank machen kann.

provenance.note: Carrier verbatim from masterlist exampleSb ('There are no deserts in England.'); SB8 ('It lives in the deserts of Australia'). Glosses 'sand'/'almost'/'rain' (d).

items.d: A very hot place with a lot of sand and almost no rain.
items.s: There are no ___ in England.
items.hintDe: Ein sehr heißer Ort mit viel Sand und fast ohne Regen.

provenance.note: Carrier invented (v1 'jump out of the sea' rephrased to in-level 'river'). SONG calls dolphins 'the smartest animals'. Glosses 'sea' and 'water' (def only).

items.d: A clever sea mammal that jumps up out of the water.
items.s: The clever ___ jumped up out of the river.
items.hintDe: Ein kluges Meeressäugetier, das aus dem Wasser springt.

provenance.note: Carrier from SB reading ('a farmer in Costa Rica saw a huge crocodile'); 'saw' above-level, so reframed with in-level 'had a big farm'.

items.d: A man or a woman who keeps pigs and horses.
items.s: A ___ near the river had a big farm.
items.hintDe: Ein Mann oder eine Frau, der/die einen Bauernhof hat.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('Cheetahs can run very fast.') + SB8 ('It can run very fast'). Definition avoids the antonym 'slow' (above-level) by using bank word 'speed'.

items.d: Going at a great speed.
items.s: Cheetahs can run very ___. They are faster than a car.
items.hintDe: Mit großer Geschwindigkeit unterwegs.

provenance.note: Carrier from SB6 golden toads fact ('The male was golden red, the female was dark green.'); 'male' is the bank partner word and appears as fixed text. Glosses 'toad'/'golden' (s) and 'animal' (d). 'male' is NOT in mc (it is the obvious wrong contrast).

items.d: A girl or a woman; the mother animal in a family.
items.s: The ___ toad is dark green; the male toad is golden red.
items.hintDe: Ein Mädchen oder eine Frau — bei Tieren das Weibchen.

provenance.note: Carrier verbatim from masterlist exampleSb ('We want to be best friends forever.').

items.d: For all time; with no end.
items.s: We want to be best friends ___.
items.hintDe: Für alle Zeit, ohne Ende.

provenance.note: Carrier from SB13 quiz ('Which is the tallest? giraffe'), phrased as a fact. Glosses 'neck' (def), 'animal' and 'world' (carrier).

items.d: The tallest mammal on Earth. It has got a very long neck and spots.
items.s: The ___ is the tallest animal in the world.
items.hintDe: Das höchste Säugetier der Welt — mit sehr langem Hals und Flecken.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('My pet rat has got lots of fur. It's very hairy.'); 'pet rat' swapped to in-level 'dog'.

items.d: With a lot of hair or fur on it.
items.s: My dog has got lots of fur. It is very ___.
items.hintDe: Mit viel Haar oder Fell am Körper.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('This bag is too heavy. I can't carry it.'), kept close ('school bag is too heavy to carry').

items.d: It weighs so much that you cannot carry it.
items.s: My school bag is too ___ to carry today.
items.hintDe: So schwer, dass du es kaum tragen kannst.

provenance.note: Carrier verbatim from SB reading ('I wanted to show the animal that not all humans are bad'); 'the animal' swapped to 'the crocodile' (in-level, same referent).

items.d: A man, a woman or a child.
items.s: I wanted to show the crocodile that not all ___ are bad.
items.hintDe: Ein Mann, eine Frau oder ein Kind — wir alle sind das.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('That's an incredible but true story.'), matching SB reading 'The incredible, but true story'.

items.d: Very, very good or very surprising.
items.s: That's an ___ but true story.
items.hintDe: So gut oder so erstaunlich, dass man es kaum glauben kann.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('Dolphins are very intelligent animals.') = SB13. 'smart' (near-synonym, a bank word) is deliberately NOT in mc. Glosses 'able'/'learn' (d), 'animals' (s).

items.d: Very good at thinking and learning.
items.s: Dolphins are very ___ animals. They can think very well.
items.hintDe: Sehr gut im Denken; kann schnell Neues lernen.

provenance.note: Carrier from SB9 fact file (anaconda max length 6 m) + masterlist ('The maximum length is 6 m.'). Glosses 'metres' (s).

items.d: How long a thing is from end to end.
items.s: An anaconda can be six metres long. That is a very big ___.
items.hintDe: Wie lang etwas ist, von einem Ende zum anderen.

provenance.note: Carrier from v1 idea, fully in-level. Masterlist exampleSb ('The rhino was fast, but less than 60 km/h.') uses above-level 'km/h', so the 'less sugar' example is used.

items.d: A smaller number; not as much.
items.s: I eat ___ sugar now because I want to be healthy.
items.hintDe: Eine kleinere Menge — nicht so viel.

provenance.note: Carrier verbatim from SB song ('What animal can bring us luck?'). Glosses 'animal' (s) and 'chance' (d).

items.d: When good things happen to you by chance.
items.s: What animal can bring us ___?
items.hintDe: Wenn dir gute Dinge durch Zufall passieren.

provenance.note: Carrier from SB6 golden toads fact ('The male was golden red'); 'female' is the bank partner word, appears as fixed text. Glosses 'toad'/'golden' (s), 'father'/'animal' (d). 'female' is NOT in mc.

items.d: A boy or a man; the father animal in a family.
items.s: The ___ toad is golden red; the female toad is dark green.
items.hintDe: Ein Junge oder ein Mann — bei Tieren das Männchen.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('Elephants and lions are mammals.') + in-level 'They give milk to their young'. Glosses 'animal'/'baby' (d).

items.d: An animal that drinks milk from its mother when it is a baby.
items.s: Elephants and lions are ___. They give milk to their young.
items.hintDe: Ein Tier, das als Baby Milch von seiner Mutter trinkt.

provenance.note: Carrier adapted from SB8 ('The most dangerous animal in the world is the MOSQUITO. It can carry malaria.'). Glosses 'insect' (def) and 'malaria' (s and d).

items.d: A tiny insect that bites you. It can carry malaria.
items.s: A tiny ___ can carry malaria when it bites you.
items.hintDe: Ein winziges Tier, das dich sticht und Malaria übertragen kann.

provenance.note: Carrier invented in-level. Masterlist/SB6 ('Nobody knows why the paradise parrot became extinct.') uses above-level 'knows'/'extinct', so a simpler park sentence is used. 'no one' accepted as a full equivalent.

items.d: No one at all.
items.s: There was ___ in the park, so I played alone.
items.hintDe: Keine einzige Person, gar niemand.

provenance.note: Carrier adapted from SB13 quiz ('Which is the tallest? a giraffe b an ostrich'); reframed as a fact. Glosses 'bird' and 'fly'.

items.d: A very big bird that cannot fly. It runs fast on its long legs.
items.s: An ___ is taller than a man.
items.hintDe: Ein sehr großer Vogel, der nicht fliegen kann, aber schnell läuft.

provenance.note: Carrier invented (v1 idea reused, rephrased to in-level words; 'say' is above-level). Glosses 'bird'.

items.d: A colourful bird that can talk like you.
items.s: The ___ at the zoo can talk just like us.
items.hintDe: Ein bunter Vogel, der sprechen kann wie du.

provenance.note: Carrier invented (v1 idea reused). Glosses 'bird' (above-level). SB6 references passenger pigeons but no usable blank.

items.d: A small grey bird that lives in a city. There are many in the park.
items.s: There are many grey ___ in the city.
items.hintDe: Ein kleiner grauer Vogel, den du oft in der Stadt und im Park siehst.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('Rhinos are very powerful animals.') + SB song ('Rhinos are so powerful'); 'push over a tree' echoes the elephant idea. 'strong' (near-synonym, bank word) deliberately NOT in mc.

items.d: Very strong; you can push over a big tree.
items.s: Rhinos are very ___. They can push over a tree.
items.hintDe: Sehr stark — kann sogar einen großen Baum umdrücken.

provenance.note: Carrier verbatim from masterlist exampleSb ('Is there a reason why you don't want to come over?'); 'over' trimmed (in-level either way).

items.d: Why a thing happens, or why you do a thing.
items.s: Is there a ___ why you don't want to come?
items.hintDe: Warum etwas passiert oder warum du etwas tust.

provenance.note: Carrier from SONG ('I'd say let's pick a rhino, bigger than a truck'). Glosses 'horn' in def.

items.d: A big strong grey mammal with a horn on its nose.
items.s: A ___ can be bigger than a truck.
items.hintDe: Ein großes starkes graues Säugetier mit einem Horn auf der Nase.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('I want to become a scientist and study animals.'). Glosses 'animals' (s and d) and 'nature' (d).

items.d: A man or a woman who studies animals and nature.
items.s: I want to study animals one day, so I want to be a ___.
items.hintDe: Ein Mann oder eine Frau, der/die Tiere und die Natur erforscht.

provenance.note: Carrier invented (v1 idea reused; 'scared'/'swimming in the sea' are in-level). SB13 lists 'whale shark' as longest. Glosses 'sea' (in s and d).

items.d: A big fish with long teeth that lives in the sea.
items.s: Some people are scared of swimming in the sea because of ___.
items.hintDe: Ein großer Fisch mit langen Zähnen, der im Meer lebt.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('Cats are very smart animals.'); 'cats'/'animals' above-level here, so reframed with in-level 'dog'. 'intelligent'/'smart' near-synonyms NOT in mc. Glosses 'learning'/'quickly' (d), 'word'/'say' (s).

items.d: Good at learning and thinking quickly.
items.s: Our new dog is very ___. He understands every word I say.
items.hintDe: Gut darin, schnell zu denken und zu lernen.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('Their speed on land is 8 km/h.'); 'km/h' above-level, so reframed with in-level 'at a very fast speed'. Definition uses bank word 'fast'.

items.d: How fast a thing can go.
items.s: A cheetah can run at a very fast ___.
items.hintDe: Wie schnell sich etwas bewegt.

provenance.note: verify r1 (answers): replaced the near-synonym distractor 'powerful' (the unit even glosses powerful as 'very strong') with 'fast'/'dangerous' — all clearly NOT strong.

items.d: With a lot of power; you can carry heavy boxes.
items.s: I'm not as ___ as my best friend. He can carry big boxes.
items.hintDe: Mit viel Kraft — du kannst schwere Kisten tragen.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('Mosquitos can carry malaria.') = SB8 fact. Def rephrased into in-level words; glosses only 'malaria' (s).

items.d: To hold a thing and bring it to a new place.
items.s: Mosquitos can ___ malaria.
items.hintDe: Etwas festhalten und woandershin bringen (hier: eine Krankheit übertragen).

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('Millions of people die from malaria.') = SB8. 'die out' (the phrase) is NOT in mc to avoid confusion with the related bank word. Glosses 'stop' (d), 'malaria' (s).

items.d: To stop living; to come to the end of life.
items.s: Many people ___ from malaria every year.
items.hintDe: Aufhören zu leben.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('Many animals are dying out.'); 'dying' (continuous) is above-level, so reframed with 'could die out'. The blank fills with 'die' (the 'out' is fixed in the sentence); full dAnswers cover all forms of the phrase.

items.d: When the last one of a kind is gone forever.
items.s: These crocodiles could ___ out one day if we don't help them.
items.hintDe: Wenn das letzte Tier einer Art für immer weg ist.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('Our stones can protect you.') reframed to the unit's conservation theme ('protect wild animals'); SB story 'Only your stones can protect you now.' Glosses 'danger' (d), 'safe' (s and d), 'animals' (s).

items.d: To keep a friend or a thing safe from danger.
items.s: We must ___ wild animals and keep them safe.
items.hintDe: Jemanden oder etwas vor Gefahr sicher halten.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('Share your notes with a partner.'); 'notes'/'partner' above-level, so reframed with in-level 'sweets'/'sister'. SB17 'Share your notes with a partner' is the source.

items.d: To give some of what you have to a friend.
items.s: Please ___ your sweets with your sister.
items.hintDe: Einem Freund etwas von dem geben, was du hast.

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb ('A python can weigh up to 100 kg.'); 'python'/'kg' above-level, so reframed with in-level 'shark'/'tons'. Definition uses sense 'find out how heavy'.

items.d: To find out how heavy a thing is.
items.s: A big shark can ___ more than two tons.
items.hintDe: Herausfinden, wie schwer etwas ist (oder ein bestimmtes Gewicht haben).

provenance.note: Carrier from masterlist/SB8 ('It weighs 150 tons.' — the blue whale). Glosses 'unit'/'weight'/'kilograms' (d).

items.d: A very heavy unit of weight: one thousand kilograms.
items.s: A whale can weigh 150 ___.
items.hintDe: Eine sehr schwere Gewichtseinheit: gleich 1000 Kilogramm.

provenance.note: Carrier verbatim from SB song ('bigger than a truck') = masterlist exampleSb. Def rephrased into in-level words; no gloss.

items.d: A very big thing for the road that can bring heavy boxes from place to place.
items.s: A rhino can be bigger than a ___.
items.hintDe: Ein sehr großes Straßenfahrzeug, das schwere Dinge transportiert.

provenance.note: Carrier from SB9/WB ('the most venomous snake') + masterlist gloss ('A venomous animal produces poison'). Glosses 'poison' (s and d) and 'snake' (s). SB itself glosses venomous = giftig.

items.d: Having poison in its bite that can make you very ill.
items.s: A ___ snake has got poison in its bite.
items.hintDe: Mit Gift im Biss, das dich sehr krank machen kann.

provenance.note: Carrier from SB8 ('The biggest animal on land or in the sea is the BLUE WHALE. It's also the heaviest.'), rephrased to in-level words with 'mammal'. Glosses 'sea' in def.

items.d: The biggest mammal on Earth. It lives in the sea.
items.s: The ___ is the biggest and heaviest mammal of all.
items.hintDe: Das größte Säugetier der Erde — es lebt im Meer.
